spec_match.rationale: The page now reads the current WICG `navigator.cpuPerformance` numeric tier, shows a legacy-draft `navigator.cpu.performance()` fallback only if present, and maps that static tier together with Compute Pressure states.

interactive.rationale: Users can inspect API and heuristic tiers side by side, override the applied tier, simulate pressure states, run an oscillation sequence, pause adaptation, and watch the chart, matrix, recommendation, and media preview update.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The live dashboard still pairs the static CPU tier with live pressure and maps tier x pressure to quality settings, matching the feature index blurb.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo handles missing CPU Performance support, PressureObserver or Permissions-Policy failures, rapid fair/serious oscillation with a 3-sample hysteresis toggle, paused adaptation, and the low-tier critical audio-only floor.

references.rationale: The see-also section now links to ChromeStatus, the CPU Performance proposal, and the Compute Pressure spec.

design_system.rationale: Raw hex and rgba canvas colors were replaced with CSS variables and computed design tokens, and local button/probe states set paired background and text colors.

summary: The compute-pressure pairing demo now uses the current CPU Performance API shape, explains the heuristic fallback, cites required references, avoids raw colors, and teaches hysteresis for noisy pressure streams.
